Management discussion and analysis

Net revenue increased 18 percent year over year to $12.0 billion, driven by stronger enterprise demand and higher cloud-related product shipments during the quarter.

Diluted EPS was $1.24 compared with $1.05 in the prior-year period, while operating expenses grew at a slower pace than revenue and supported margin expansion.

Our outlook for the next quarter assumes continued demand from large customers, with guidance for revenue growth in the mid-teens and stable gross margin.

Segment results improved in data center and software, while the consumer segment remained under pressure from channel inventory normalization.

Risk factors include forward-looking statements, customer concentration, supply constraints, foreign exchange volatility, and changes in end-market demand.
